BigShot_Spike.NAME: Big Shot!
DESCRIPTIONS: A battered monitor flickers to life. Punch and Judy are mid-broadcast on Big Shot!, rattling off a bounty in this sector. High risk. High reward. Just the way Spike likes it.
You jot down the mark's last known location. The Woolong will spend nicely.
You click off the screen. Not worth the trouble today.

ToysInTheAttic_Spike.NAME: Toys in the Attic
DESCRIPTIONS: Something shifts inside a sealed container. Nobody put it there — or nobody remembers. The smell is wrong. The shape is wrong. But there might be something valuable buried underneath.
You reach inside. Bad idea. Whatever bit you retreats into the dark. You feel weaker, but your hand closes around something useful.
You back away slowly and leave the container sealed. Some things are better left alone.
OPTIONS: Look Inside [Lose 15% Max HP, Gain a Rare Card]

JupiterJazz_Spike.NAME: Jupiter Jazz
DESCRIPTIONS: On a frozen street on Callisto, a lone saxophonist plays into the cold air. Gren — soldier, smuggler, something else now. The music is beautiful and sad in equal measure.
You stand in the cold and listen until the last note fades. The melody stays with you — its rhythm becomes a card in your deck.
You buy a round for the house. Gren shares the bottle, and for a while the warmth of company knits you back together.
You turn up your collar and walk on. The music fades behind you.
OPTIONS: Listen to the Music [Lose 8 HP, Add Jupiter Jazz to your deck]
Buy a Round [Lose 50 Gold, Heal 15 HP]

BellPeppersAndBeef_Spike.NAME: Bell Peppers and Beef
DESCRIPTIONS: Jet sets a steaming plate on the galley table - bell peppers and beef, his specialty. Except there's no beef again; the Bebop's flat broke. NL "Eat up," he grunts. "It's good for you."
You clear the plate. Jet's cooking - peppers and all - somehow hits the spot. You feel a little more human.
You drop your last Woolong on the table and Jet actually springs for the beef. A real meal for once, and you feel genuinely restored.
"Suit yourself." Jet shrugs and covers the plate. Hunger's a feeling you're used to.
